chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.S-181 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections at flag "A". 2. For hearing of main case. ------ 10.07.2025 Mr. Muhammad Murad Tunio, Advocate for the Petitioner a/w Petitioner. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Bhutto, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh Mr. Ali Anwar Kandhro, Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh a/w Inspector Noor Ahmed Mugheri, S.H.O. P.S. Veehar, DSP Imdad Ali Talpur, SDPO, Dokri, SIP Ali Muhammad Bhatti, Additional S.H.O. P.S. Fateh Pur and SIP Ali Dost on behalf of S.S.P. Larkana. ------ Mr. Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, advocate files vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.7, 8, 9, 12 and 13, who are also present in person. Vakalatnama is taken on record. It appears that the dispute between the parties is over landed property viz. Survey No.182 and 183, situated in Deh Khacharpur, Tapo Budhodero, Taluka Dokri and both the parties claim ownership of the said land; however, such factual controversy cannot be resolved by this Court in its writ jurisdiction being barred by Article 212 of the Constitution, Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. Nonetheless, both the parties are at liberty to file proper application/suit before the appropriate forum, if so advised. As far as harassment is concerned, the official respondents, present in the Court, have categorically stated that they have neither caused harassment to the petitioner or his family members nor intend to do so in future except due course of law. Learned counsel for the private respondents also submits that his clients have not harassed the petitioner and submits that the private respondents are also holding title documents of the said property.

As far as harassment is concerned, the official respondents, present in the Court, have categorically stated that they have neither caused harassment to the petitioner or his family members nor intend to do so in future except due course of law. Learned counsel for the private respondents also submits that his clients have not harassed the petitioner and submits that the private respondents are also holding title documents of the said property. In view of above, this petition is hereby disposed of with directions to both the parties to approach the appropriate forum by filing proper applications for redressal of their grievance, if so advised. Judge Manzoor
